data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <bead id="ddx-31fba984">
    <title>artifacts: workflow-stage axis + paginated count wording + sessionStorage persistence</title>
    <description>
Add Workflow Stage as a group-by axis (derived from generic <NN>-<slug> path regex; hidden when no artifacts have a matching path). Group counts under pagination show 'of N loaded' to be honest about page-locality. sessionStorage persists groupBy across navigations.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. Workflow Stage axis available when applicable; hidden otherwise. 2. Group counts label honestly: 'X of N loaded' under pagination. 3. sessionStorage round-trip works.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, story:5, area:web, kind:feature</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-ffb678fc" base-rev="85bc44a04e831fc5ca9453294ac0cac54f2221ef" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260505T082544-0ac6873d"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260505T082544-0ac6873d/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260505T082544-0ac6873d/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260505T082544-0ac6873d/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260505T082544-0ac6873d/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260505T082544-0ac6873d/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Implemented and committed as `2e639698` (`feat: add artifacts workflow stage grouping [ddx-31fba984]`).

What changed:
- Added `workflowStage` as a first-class artifacts group-by axis, with stage derivation and fallback grouping in [`grouping.ts`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-31fba984-20260505T082544-0ac6873d/cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/%5BnodeId%5D/projects/%5BprojectId%5D/artifacts/grouping.ts).
- Hid the Workflow stage option when no loaded artifacts have a matching stage path, and added honest pagination counts like `X of N loaded` in [`+page.svelte`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-31fba984-20260505T082544-0ac6873d/cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/%5BnodeId%5D/projects/%5BprojectId%5D/artifacts/%2Bpage.svelte).
- Added sessionStorage round-trip helpers and coverage in [`persistence.ts`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-31fba984-20260505T082544-0ac6873d/cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/%5BnodeId%5D/projects/%5BprojectId%5D/artifacts/persistence.ts) and [`persistence.test.ts`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-31fba984-20260505T082544-0ac6873d/cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/%5BnodeId%5D/projects/%5BprojectId%5D/artifacts/persistence.test.ts).
- Extended URL-state parsing/writing so `groupBy=workflowStage` survives navigation in [`urlState.ts`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-31fba984-20260505T082544-0ac6873d/cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/urlState.ts).

Verification:
- `bun x vitest run src/lib/urlState.test.ts 'src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/grouping.test.ts' 'src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/persistence.test.ts' 'src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/searchFetcher.test.ts'`
- `bun x eslint 'src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/+page.svelte'`
- `bun x eslint src/lib/urlState.ts src/lib/urlState.test.ts 'src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/+page.ts' 'src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/grouping.ts' 'src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/grouping.test.ts' 'src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/persistence.ts' 'src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/persistence.test.ts'`
- `bun x prettier --check ...` on the touched files
- `bun run check` passed with existing repo warnings only
- `lefthook run pre-commit` passed

One note: the full frontend `bun run lint` / `bun run test:unit -- --run` still surfaces unrelated pre-existing repo issues outside this bead, so I validated the touched files directly and kept the commit scoped to the artifacts work.
